speeches.name: Jerome Powell
speeches.summary: Powell said the FOMC held rates steady, judged the current stance appropriate, and emphasized balancing downside labor-market risks against upside inflation risks.

speeches.name: Susan M. Collins
speeches.summary: Collins discussed the economic outlook and labor market, a topic that typically informs policy but the available result does not provide enough content to classify the tone precisely.

speeches.name: Christopher J. Waller
speeches.summary: Powell's March press conference repeated that policy was appropriately restrictive enough to support progress on inflation and employment, while saying the Committee was not cutting rates at that meeting.

speeches.name: Jerome Powell
speeches.summary: Powell reiterated that the Fed had already lowered rates in late 2025 and was keeping policy in a range consistent with neutrality while inflation trends toward 2%.

macro_fred.narrative: Leaning Neutral: CPI at 4.0%, Fed Funds at 3.62%, unemployment at 4.3%.
